Francis Uzoho.bio: A young goalkeeper who debuted for Nigeria as a teenager and has since gained experience across Europe, including stints in Spain, Greece, and Cyprus. Uzoho's shot-stopping ability has kept him in the national team picture despite playing at a lower-profile club.
Francis Uzoho.playingStyle: Tall, athletic goalkeeper with excellent reflexes and the ability to make spectacular saves, though occasionally prone to lapses in concentration.
Francis Uzoho.worldCup2026Role: Goalkeeper option providing competition and the ability to produce match-winning saves when called upon.
Francis Uzoho.notableFact: Became one of the youngest goalkeepers to start a World Cup match when he played for Nigeria at the 2018 World Cup at just 19 years old.

Ola Aina.bio: A versatile defender who came through Chelsea's academy before building his career at Torino in Serie A and then Nottingham Forest in the Premier League. Aina can play on either side of defense and brings technical quality rare for a fullback.
Ola Aina.playingStyle: Technically gifted, two-footed fullback capable of playing on either side, combining dribbling ability with tactical intelligence and solid defensive work.
Ola Aina.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back who provides technical quality, defensive solidity, and the ability to contribute in the buildup play from the back.
Ola Aina.notableFact: Born in London and came through Chelsea's academy alongside the likes of Mason Mount before choosing to represent Nigeria internationally.

Calvin Bassey.bio: A powerful left-footed center-back who rose to prominence at Rangers, earning a move to Ajax and then Fulham in the Premier League. Bassey is one of the most physically gifted defenders in Nigerian football history.
Calvin Bassey.playingStyle: Explosive, athletic center-back with blistering pace for his position, strong in the tackle, and capable of carrying the ball forward to start attacks.
Calvin Bassey.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back and one of the cornerstones of Nigeria's defense, using his pace and power to shut down opposing attackers.
Calvin Bassey.notableFact: Was named in the Europa League Team of the Season for his performances in Rangers' run to the 2022 final before his move to Ajax.

William Ekong.bio: One of Nigeria's most experienced defenders, Ekong has been a mainstay of the national team for years. He has played in Belgium, Italy, England, and Saudi Arabia, accumulating over 70 caps and serving as captain.
William Ekong.playingStyle: Experienced, intelligent center-back who reads the game well and uses his positioning and organization to compensate for any physical limitations.
William Ekong.worldCup2026Role: Veteran defensive leader providing experience, captaincy material, and the ability to organize the backline in high-pressure situations.
William Ekong.notableFact: Has captained Nigeria on numerous occasions and was part of the squad that reached the 2024 AFCON final.

Semi Ajayi.bio: A towering center-back who has been a standout performer for West Bromwich Albion in the Championship. Ajayi also has experience in the Premier League and offers an aerial presence that few can match.
Semi Ajayi.playingStyle: Dominant aerial presence who excels in both defensive and attacking set-piece situations, combining height with surprising agility and mobility.
Semi Ajayi.worldCup2026Role: Center-back depth option who provides an aerial weapon from set pieces and defensive solidity when called upon.
Semi Ajayi.notableFact: Has scored multiple goals from corners and set pieces for both club and country, making him a genuine weapon from dead-ball situations.

Wilfred Ndidi.bio: One of the best defensive midfielders in African football, Ndidi spent years as a key player at Leicester City in the Premier League before moving to Besiktas. His ball-winning ability and tactical discipline make him the anchor of Nigeria's midfield.
Wilfred Ndidi.playingStyle: Elite ball-winner who combines relentless tackling and interceptions with the discipline to maintain his position and allow others to attack. A midfield destroyer in the truest sense.
Wilfred Ndidi.worldCup2026Role: First-choice defensive midfielder who provides the platform for Nigeria's attacking talents by winning the ball and keeping things simple.
Wilfred Ndidi.notableFact: Led the Premier League in tackles per game for multiple seasons at Leicester City and was a key part of their historic FA Cup triumph in 2021.

Paul Onuachu.bio: A towering striker standing 6'7" who has been a prolific goalscorer in Belgium and Turkey. Onuachu's height and aerial ability make him one of the most unique attacking threats in African football.
Paul Onuachu.playingStyle: Extremely tall target man who dominates in the air, using his height to win headers, hold up play, and score from crosses and set pieces.
Paul Onuachu.worldCup2026Role: Specialist substitute who provides a unique aerial threat for late-game situations or when Nigeria need a physical presence up front.
Paul Onuachu.notableFact: At 6'7" (201cm), he is one of the tallest outfield players in international football and was the Belgian Pro League top scorer in 2020-21 with 33 goals.

Frank Onyeka.bio: An energetic midfielder who has been a regular at Brentford since their promotion to the Premier League. Onyeka brings tireless running, pressing, and box-to-box energy to Nigeria's midfield.
Frank Onyeka.playingStyle: High-energy box-to-box midfielder who covers immense ground, presses opponents relentlessly, and contributes in both defensive and attacking phases with his running power.
Frank Onyeka.worldCup2026Role: Midfield option providing energy and pressing ability, useful as a starter or high-intensity substitute.
Frank Onyeka.notableFact: Was part of the Brentford team that gained promotion to the Premier League and has established himself as a regular in England's top flight.

Victor Osimhen.bio: Nigeria's superstar striker and one of the most sought-after forwards in world football. Osimhen rose from humble beginnings in Lagos to become Serie A's top scorer at Napoli, leading them to their first league title in 33 years. Currently on loan at Galatasaray, he is the most valuable Nigerian player in history.
Victor Osimhen.playingStyle: Explosive, relentless striker who terrorizes defenders with his pace, movement, aerial ability, and powerful finishing. A complete modern center forward who combines physical dominance with intelligent runs.
Victor Osimhen.worldCup2026Role: Nigeria's talisman and the focal point of their entire attack. Every tactical plan revolves around getting the best out of Osimhen.
Victor Osimhen.notableFact: Won the Serie A title and Capocannoniere (top scorer) with Napoli in 2022-23, becoming the first Nigerian to win the Italian league's Golden Boot.

Joe Aribo.bio: A versatile midfielder who made his name at Rangers in Scotland before moving to Southampton and then Leicester City. Aribo can play as an attacking midfielder, central midfielder, or even as a forward, offering tactical flexibility.
Joe Aribo.playingStyle: Silky, technically gifted midfielder with the ability to glide past opponents, play intricate passes, and contribute goals from advanced positions.
Joe Aribo.worldCup2026Role: Versatile attacking midfield option who can operate as a number 10 or deeper, providing creativity and tactical flexibility.
Joe Aribo.notableFact: Scored in the 2022 Europa League final for Rangers against Eintracht Frankfurt and was named in the Scottish Premiership Team of the Season.

Samuel Chukwueze.bio: An exciting right winger who burst onto the scene as a teenager at Villarreal before earning a move to AC Milan. Chukwueze is known for his speed, direct dribbling, and left-footed goal threat cutting in from the right.
Samuel Chukwueze.playingStyle: Pacy, inverted right winger who cuts inside onto his left foot to shoot or create, using rapid changes of direction and explosive acceleration to beat defenders.
keyStrengths: Cutting inside on his left foot
Samuel Chukwueze.worldCup2026Role: Starting right winger providing pace, width, and a goal threat from the flank to complement Osimhen centrally.
Samuel Chukwueze.notableFact: Scored the semi-final winning goal for Villarreal against Liverpool in the 2021-22 Champions League, one of the competition's biggest upsets.

Maduka Okoye.bio: A German-born Nigerian goalkeeper who chose to represent Nigeria and has played at Sparta Rotterdam, Watford, and Udinese in Serie A. Okoye has emerged as a strong contender for the number one jersey.
Maduka Okoye.playingStyle: Modern goalkeeper comfortable with the ball at his feet, strong in one-on-one situations, and capable of making athletic saves at full stretch.
Maduka Okoye.worldCup2026Role: Leading candidate for starting goalkeeper, providing modern goalkeeping with strong distribution and shot-stopping.
Maduka Okoye.notableFact: Born in Germany and played in the Dutch and English leagues before establishing himself in Serie A with Udinese.

Bright Osayi-Samuel.bio: A dynamic wing-back who has reinvented himself at Fenerbahce in Turkey after starting his career as a winger at QPR. Osayi-Samuel's pace and attacking instinct make him one of the most exciting fullbacks in Nigerian football.
Bright Osayi-Samuel.playingStyle: Attack-minded wing-back with electric pace, skillful dribbling from deep positions, and the ability to beat opponents one-on-one before delivering dangerous crosses.
Bright Osayi-Samuel.worldCup2026Role: Dynamic right-back or wing-back option who provides an attacking alternative with his pace and skill from deep positions.
Bright Osayi-Samuel.notableFact: Converted from a winger to a wing-back at Fenerbahce and has become one of the best in the Turkish Super Lig in that position.

Kelechi Iheanacho.bio: A natural goalscorer who came through Manchester City's academy before building a strong career at Leicester City and then Celtic. Iheanacho has been one of Nigeria's most reliable forwards over the past decade.
Kelechi Iheanacho.playingStyle: Clever, instinctive striker who excels in and around the penalty area, using his anticipation, quick feet, and natural finishing ability to score goals.
keyStrengths: Ability to score in big moments
Kelechi Iheanacho.worldCup2026Role: Experienced backup striker who can partner or replace Osimhen, providing a proven goal threat and big-game experience.
Kelechi Iheanacho.notableFact: Won the Premier League Golden Boot for the month of March 2021 with Leicester City, scoring crucial goals in their FA Cup run.

Moses Simon.bio: One of Nigeria's most consistent performers over the past several years, Simon has been a regular at Nantes in Ligue 1. Despite his diminutive stature at just 5'4", his pace and skill make him a nightmare for defenders.
Moses Simon.playingStyle: Diminutive but devastatingly quick winger who uses his low center of gravity, rapid feet, and pace to beat defenders and create chances from the left side.
Moses Simon.worldCup2026Role: Starting or rotation left winger providing pace, directness, and creativity from the flank.
Moses Simon.notableFact: At just 5'4" (162cm), he is one of the shortest players in the World Cup but compensates with some of the quickest feet in African football.

Taiwo Awoniyi.bio: A powerful striker who endured numerous loan spells from Liverpool before finally settling at Union Berlin and then Nottingham Forest. Awoniyi's perseverance paid off as he became a regular Premier League goalscorer.
Taiwo Awoniyi.playingStyle: Strong, physical striker who excels at holding the ball up, winning aerial duels, and finishing inside the box with power and composure.
Taiwo Awoniyi.worldCup2026Role: Backup striker providing a physical, direct alternative to Osimhen with his hold-up play and aerial threat.
Taiwo Awoniyi.notableFact: Went on seven loan spells from Liverpool before finding his home at Union Berlin, where he scored 15 Bundesliga goals in his debut season.

Alex Iwobi.bio: Born in Lagos and raised in London, Iwobi came through Arsenal's academy and has evolved into one of the Premier League's most versatile midfielders at Everton and then Fulham. His tactical intelligence and technical ability make him indispensable to Nigeria.
Alex Iwobi.playingStyle: Intelligent, technically excellent midfielder who can play in multiple positions, excelling at carrying the ball, finding teammates in space, and maintaining possession under pressure.
Alex Iwobi.worldCup2026Role: Key midfield starter who provides the technical quality, intelligence, and link-up play to connect Nigeria's defense with their dangerous attack.
Alex Iwobi.notableFact: Nephew of legendary Nigerian striker Jay-Jay Okocha and has evolved from an Arsenal winger into one of the best central midfielders in the Premier League.

Zaidu Sanusi.bio: A left-back who has become a regular at Porto in Portugal, contributing to multiple Primeira Liga title challenges. Sanusi's pace and defensive ability have made him Nigeria's first-choice left-back.
Zaidu Sanusi.playingStyle: Pacy left-back who excels at recovery runs and defending one-on-one, providing defensive solidity while also getting forward to support attacks down the left flank.
Zaidu Sanusi.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back providing pace, defensive reliability, and experience from competing at the highest levels with Porto.
Zaidu Sanusi.notableFact: Scored a dramatic stoppage-time winner for Porto against AC Milan in the 2021-22 Champions League group stage.

Ademola Lookman.bio: The 2024 CAF African Player of the Year, Lookman has become one of the best attackers in European football at Atalanta in Serie A. Born in London, he chose Nigeria over England and has become the team's most in-form attacker alongside Osimhen.
Ademola Lookman.playingStyle: Explosive, two-footed winger who can play on either flank, combining blistering pace with exceptional dribbling, clinical finishing, and the ability to produce moments of individual brilliance.
Ademola Lookman.worldCup2026Role: Key attacking player alongside Osimhen, expected to start on the left wing and be one of Nigeria's most dangerous creative and goalscoring threats.
Ademola Lookman.notableFact: Scored a hat-trick in the 2024 Europa League final for Atalanta against Bayer Leverkusen and won the 2024 African Player of the Year award.

Terem Moffi.bio: A powerful striker who has been a consistent goalscorer in Ligue 1, particularly at Lorient and then Nice. Moffi's physical attributes and finishing ability make him a useful option in Nigeria's deep forward pool.
Terem Moffi.playingStyle: Strong, physical striker who combines pace with power, capable of leading the line alone or playing alongside another forward with his direct running and finishing.
Terem Moffi.worldCup2026Role: Backup forward option who provides a physical, direct alternative from the bench when Nigeria need to change the dynamic of an attack.
Terem Moffi.notableFact: Was one of the top-scoring African players in Ligue 1 during his time at Lorient, earning his move to Nice.

Bruno Onyemaechi.bio: A young left-back who has developed in Portuguese football at Boavista. Onyemaechi represents the next generation of Nigerian fullbacks and brings energy and defensive commitment to the squad.
Bruno Onyemaechi.playingStyle: Energetic young left-back who is defensively solid and willing to get forward, using his stamina and pace to cover the full length of the left flank.
Bruno Onyemaechi.worldCup2026Role: Young backup left-back providing fresh legs and competition at the position behind Zaidu Sanusi.
Bruno Onyemaechi.notableFact: Has been one of the standout young Nigerian players in Portuguese football and represents the growing pipeline of Nigerian talent in the Primeira Liga.

Stanley Nwabali.bio: A goalkeeper who became a national hero during the 2024 Africa Cup of Nations with a series of stunning performances, including crucial penalty saves. Despite playing in the South African league, his AFCON heroics earned him widespread recognition.
Stanley Nwabali.playingStyle: Brave, confident goalkeeper with excellent penalty-saving ability and the temperament to perform in high-pressure situations despite his lower-profile club career.
Stanley Nwabali.worldCup2026Role: Goalkeeper option who brings big-tournament experience from his AFCON heroics and proven penalty-saving ability.
Stanley Nwabali.notableFact: Saved multiple penalties during the 2024 AFCON to help Nigeria reach the final, becoming a national sensation despite playing in the South African league.

Chidozie Awaziem.bio: A versatile defender who has played across Europe in Portugal, Turkey, and France. Awaziem can operate at center-back or right-back and has been a regular in the Nigerian squad for several years.
Chidozie Awaziem.playingStyle: Versatile, composed defender who reads the game well and can slot into multiple defensive positions without disrupting the team's organization.
Chidozie Awaziem.worldCup2026Role: Experienced defensive utility player providing cover at center-back and right-back.
Chidozie Awaziem.notableFact: Has represented Nigeria at two AFCON tournaments and multiple World Cup qualifying campaigns, providing reliable depth across the backline.

Fisayo Dele-Bashiru.bio: An exciting young midfielder who moved from Sheffield Wednesday to Lazio in Serie A, making a remarkable leap in quality. Dele-Bashiru's athleticism and box-to-box ability have made him one of the most promising young Nigerian midfielders.
Fisayo Dele-Bashiru.playingStyle: Athletic, dynamic box-to-box midfielder who combines powerful running with improving technical ability, capable of contributing in both penalty areas.
Fisayo Dele-Bashiru.worldCup2026Role: Young midfield option providing energy, physicality, and a goal threat from central positions as a rotation player or impact substitute.
Fisayo Dele-Bashiru.notableFact: Made the remarkable jump from the English Championship with Sheffield Wednesday directly to Serie A with Lazio.

Raphael Onyedika.bio: A young defensive midfielder who has impressed at Club Brugge in the Belgian First Division and Champions League. Onyedika's composure and ball-winning ability have drawn comparisons to the best Nigerian midfielders of previous generations.
Raphael Onyedika.playingStyle: Calm, composed defensive midfielder who breaks up play efficiently, distributes the ball cleanly, and provides a stable platform for the team to build attacks.
Raphael Onyedika.worldCup2026Role: Backup defensive midfielder who can deputize for Ndidi or provide a different, more composed midfield presence.
Raphael Onyedika.notableFact: Has been a key player for Club Brugge in the Champions League, performing impressively against some of Europe's biggest clubs.

Victor Boniface.bio: One of the breakout stars of European football, Boniface exploded onto the scene at Bayer Leverkusen, playing a crucial role in their unbeaten Bundesliga title-winning season. His combination of strength, technique, and finishing make him one of the most exciting Nigerian talents in a generation.
Victor Boniface.playingStyle: Powerful, technically skilled striker who combines physical dominance with silky close control, creative link-up play, and clinical finishing from any angle.
Victor Boniface.worldCup2026Role: Key striker who can start alongside or in place of Osimhen, giving Nigeria an embarrassment of riches in attack with his unique blend of power and finesse.
Victor Boniface.notableFact: Was a key part of Bayer Leverkusen's historic unbeaten Bundesliga title-winning season in 2023-24, the first in the club's history.
